title: Food & Restaurants
shortSummary: Order food, ask for the bill, and navigate a Korean restaurant with confidence.
culturalNote: Korean dining is communal and shaped by table etiquette. Meals centre on rice (밥, bap) with shared side dishes called 반찬 (banchan) that are refilled for free, plus a main dish everyone reaches into. To call a server you say 여기요 (yeogiyo, over here) or 저기요 (jeogiyo), which is normal and not rude. Respect for elders runs through the meal: you wait for the oldest person to lift their spoon first, and when pouring drinks for someone senior you use two hands and turn slightly away to drink. The most useful courtesy phrase is 잘 먹겠습니다 (jal meokgesseumnida, I will eat well) before eating and 잘 먹었습니다 (jal meogeosseumnida, I ate well) afterwards, said to whoever provided the meal. Spice level varies, so it is common to ask 안 매워요? (an maewoyo, is it not spicy?) before ordering.

keyPhrases.english: Please pack it to take away

grammar.explanation: To ask for something politely, attach 주세요 (juseyo, please give) after the item. 물 (mul, water) becomes 물 주세요. It works for objects and, with a verb stem plus 아/어, for actions.

examples.en: Please pack it to take away.

grammar.commonMistake: Dropping 주세요 and just naming the food, which sounds like a demand rather than a request.

grammar.explanation: When ordering quantities of food, use native Korean numbers after the dish: 비빔밥 하나 (bibimbap hana, one bibimbap), 커피 둘 (keopi dul, two coffees). The number follows the noun.

examples.native: 맥주 세 병 주세요. (maekju se byeong juseyo.)

grammar.commonMistake: Putting the number before the dish; in Korean the dish comes first, then the quantity.

dialogue.setup: Tom and a server at a Korean restaurant during a busy lunch.

lines.speaker: 톰
lines.english: Excuse me, please give me the menu.

lines.speaker: 종업원
lines.english: Yes, here it is. What can I get you?

lines.speaker: 톰
lines.english: One bibimbap, please. Is it not spicy?

lines.speaker: 종업원
lines.english: It's a little spicy. Is that okay?

lines.speaker: 톰
lines.english: Yes, it's fine. Some water too, please.

lines.speaker: 종업원
lines.english: Yes, one moment. Enjoy your meal.

lines.speaker: 톰
lines.english: Thank you. I will eat well!

quiz.question: How do you say 'The bill, please' in Korean?
quiz.explanation: 계산서 주세요 (gyesanseo juseyo) asks for the bill politely.

quiz.question: What do you say to call a server in a Korean restaurant?
quiz.explanation: 여기요 (yeogiyo) or 저기요 means 'over here' and is the normal way to call a server.

quiz.question: 물 좀 _____. (please give)
quiz.explanation: 주세요 (juseyo) means 'please give', so 물 좀 주세요 asks for water.

quiz.question: Which phrase is said politely before eating?
quiz.explanation: 잘 먹겠습니다 (jal meokgesseumnida) is said before a meal; 잘 먹었습니다 is said after.

quiz.question: Arrange: 주세요 / 하나 / 비빔밥 (one bibimbap, please)
quiz.explanation: Dish, then quantity, then the request: 비빔밥 하나 주세요.
